text:
Princess Anne has officially arrived in Paris for the 2024 Summer Olympics days after it was revealed her daughter Zara Tindall failed to make Team Great Britain’s squad.

Princess Anne has touched down in Paris to support Great Britain’s Olympics effort days after her daughter Zara Tindall was left off Team GB’s equestrian squad.

The equestrian portion of the 2024 Games will take place on the grounds of the historic Palace of Versailles just outside Paris.

The British Olympics committee have confirmed the Eventing team representing Great Britain in the French capital will be Rosalind Canter, Laura Collett, Tom McEwen, and Yasmin Ingham, withno spot for Tindall.

Tindall, 43, is one of the United Kingdom’s leading equestrians and previously won a silver medal during the 2012 London Olympics in Team Eventing.

However, Anne’s daughter has struggled to return to the Olympics since 2012 due to injuries to her horses.

While Anne will not be cheering on her daughter this year, the Princess Royal will be front and centre at the Olympics as president of the British Olympic Association and a member of the International Olympic Committee (IOC).

On Tuesday, Anne attended the 142nd session of the IOC at the Palais des Congrès de Paris before the global games will begin on Friday.

Anne reunited with fellow royals Prince Albert of Monaco and Grand Duke Henri of Luxembourg for the IOC group photo.

Anne, 73, stood in the back row of the photo towards the right side of the image, which marked her first overseas engagement since a terrifying horse injury.

The Princess Royal, a keen horsewoman and former equestrian, is understood to have been kicked by a horse while out for an evening stroll on the grounds of her Gatcombe Park estate last month.

Anne spent five days in hospital after suffering a concussion and possible head injuries, but the hardworking royal has already returned to official duties.

The Princess Royal will stay in France for 12 days during the games and will likely be popping up in the front row to cheer on the UK’s athletes.

Anne herself competed in the 1976 Montreal Olympics as an equestrian but missed out on a medal after falling off her horse Goodwill and cracking a vertebrae.

Meanwhile, her first husband Captain Phillips won two gold medals at the 1972 Munich Games and 1988 Seoul Games in Team Eventing.

title: Princess Anne arrives in Paris for 2024 Olympics after daughter Zara Tindall cut from Great Britain’s equestrian squad | Sky News Australia
description: Princess Anne has touched down in Paris to support Great Britain’s Olympics effort days after her daughter Zara Tindall was left off Team GB’s equestrian squad.
